What I can hear is
the voice and the faint sound of the air conditioning. It’s eerily quiet, like the outside commotion is a lie.
There’s no sign of anyone visiting, and the phone remains silent. But for me right now, that’s actually convenient.
It’s the usual dim office with the evening sun shining in. The flickering fluorescent light is painful to the eyes.
I’m not as shocked as I thought I would be. In fact, I feel somewhat more energized than usual. Are they trying to cheer me up?
Those mischievous, lively black eyes are glued to my face. It’s as if they’re keeping a watchful eye on me, worried I might just off myself.
It's great to see you lively. However,
in those cases, it tends to be more efficient to work when feeling a bit unwell.
Boss, you surprisingly look pretty good.
It's because I'm eating properly.
Alright, that's good. You need to eat; otherwise, your body won't hold up.
I was a bit worried you might feel more down, but,
it seems your little reprimand worked.
Thanks to that,
Ah.
please express your gratitude with a cheerful smile.
Hey.
There's no time to be feeling down. Boss still has things to do.
Boss, you're okay, right?
Huh?
Let's tighten our feelings and catch the murderer of Guy.
They say Guy's death was either an accident or a love-suicide.
We still don't know that. Also, I hate to say this, but he could be the person behind previous incidents.
I understand, I know how you feel, Boss, but we can’t dismiss the possibility.
Boss, let’s do our best now to find the truth.
Tony says it’s weird that Guy and Kristy went on a trip together and are getting married.
Oh really? Why's that? Even with some complications, they're engaged; it’s a natural progression, right?
No, apparently it seems Guy himself said something like that.
If that's the case, then why did they go on a trip together?
Boss, it must have just been the mood at that time. Maybe Guy was just feeling that way back then?
And did he feel differently later?
It's probably just a little lovers' quarrel. Don’t overthink it.
Boss, you shouldn't be swayed.
I think Kristy was probably happy.
You mean because you were with Gai, right?
That's right! You must have been absolutely happy.
There were a lot of things, right, Kristy?
It was tough going through counseling, but it was good that Gai was next to you in the end.
Yeah, he must have been really happy.
Sure. Besides, you were properly married to Gai.
It’s a silver lining in misfortune.
Kristy, you could have been even happier from now on.
I haven't seen her since the party.
I wonder if Meryl is feeling better now? She was saying she wanted to kill Gai back then.
I think by now, her feelings should have settled down.
That’s true.
Meryl hasn't died or anything, right?
It’s been a while since I saw Meryl.
Could it be since the party incident?
Yeah. Apparently, she went on a healing trip.
A trip, huh?
I’m glad Meryl is safe.
I wonder how Meryl feels about Gai’s death?
You made up with Jennifer, right?
What’s that? Mind your own business.
Come on, that’s not a line for someone blushing.
You need to protect Jennifer, you know.
Does Günter do counseling for things other than mental issues?
What’s that about? Counseling is just dealing with simple stress.
Jennifer must be under a lot of stress, too.
She’s an actress, so she probably deals with fierce competition and is friends with stress.
It’s not that rare to go through counseling.
I wonder if the director has made up with Patricia?
Speaking of which, that was the story. But, you know, father-daughter relationships are tough.
Even the great Director Fabar was just a normal dad.
Director, it seems we can't get in touch with Patricia. She's been really worried.
Could it be that the fight escalated?
Yeah, that might be part of it.
Supposedly, Guy and the others are in Colorado Springs where the accident happened, which makes it worse.
Huh.
Hmm, it's the same place, huh.
I have a bad feeling about this.
I'm sure everything will be okay, Patricia. They might be in Boston by now.
I was suspicious that either Guy or Kristy was the culprit. But that was a misunderstanding.
I can't say for sure whether it was a misunderstanding. Just because Guy and the others are dead doesn't mean that.
Even if they were the culprits, if this accident was a suicide...
That would mean I pulled the trigger.
So what are you getting at?
Stop acting like a tragic hero. I really don't want to see that side of you, Boss.
The culprit is still alive and well, while Guy and the others might have been killed by that culprit.
That's just...
As long as there's a possibility, we have to do everything we can.
After all, I've got nothing but time.
Time is the least of our worries.
Hehe, sorry.
Boss, please do your best.
As a professional athlete, you could still make it, and even married the woman you love. If it's an accident, that's one thing, but suicide just doesn't sit right with me.
If Guy is a serial killer, that would mean there will never be another incident.
Was it an accident or a suicide?
Or is it possible that someone killed them, making it look like an accident?
She was able to marry the man she loved, whom she protected at the cost of her own life. Surely, she was happy until the moment of her death.
What if the deaths of the two were accidental and the cause lay in Kristy's illness? Suddenly struck by a seizure,
wouldn’t it be a desecration of Kristy to imagine more about her death?
I haven't seen Meryl in a while. Even if I did see her, I'd probably be at a loss for words.
Were Meryl and the director in contact with each other?
It seems somewhat surprising, but given their long relationship, it makes sense.
Meryl appeared to have a look of resolution on her face.
Meryl didn’t even try to hide her animosity towards Guy. That attitude she had when I brought up Guy.
It annoyed me a bit.
I can’t help but think it wouldn't be strange if Meryl was behind Guy's accident.
Jennifer had previously been consulted by the director regarding Patricia, but this time she hasn’t given any advice?
Kristy and Jennifer must have crossed paths a few times. I wonder if Jennifer is in shock.
Indeed, in today's world, seeing a psychiatrist is completely normal.
I wonder what diagnosis was given?
Is Jennifer suffering from a serious illness? Or maybe it’s really nothing at all?
I have no experience of having a daughter, so I can’t say, but it seems utterly ridiculous to see the director being tossed around by Patricia.
Compared to murder or work issues, a daughter's behavior doesn't seem like a big deal.
It seems I've been quite worried about Patricia, so maybe I should check on her.
If Guy and Kristy are dead, does that mean the murders are over and they were indeed the culprits?
Since Cindy's death, there have been no new victims. Instead, there are absolutely no clues regarding the culprit or any leads on solving the case.
I wonder if this will put an end to the murders.
※※※ It's a bug ※※※
